title: tsx-eval-reachable modules must not statically import the SDK root barrel for VALUES — it silently breaks the orchestrator's tsx -e verification path
description: @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent's package.json exports map has only "." + "./hooks" (no tools/core subpath) and the root barrel has no resolvable exports main under `tsx -e` -> ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED. A static VALUE import of the root barrel in any module reachable from context-sdk / block-api / context (the tsx-eval-reachable graph) therefore breaks `npx tsx -e "...validateContext/contextState..."` — the orchestrator's primary verification path — while npm build / npm test (tsx --test) / the in-pi extension all still resolve (so the gate stays green and the breakage is silent). Occurred 2026-05-25 when FGAP-103's read-element.ts imported truncateHead from the root barrel (read-element <- context-sdk). Type-only root-barrel imports (e.g. index.ts ExtensionAPI) are erased at compile and are harmless. Recurrence of the class documented in analysis/2026-05-10-tsx-barrel-pi-coding-agent-flake.md.

evidence.reference: exports map = { ".": ..., "./hooks": ... } — no tools/core subpath; root barrel unresolvable under tsx -e

the regression: static `import { truncateHead } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent"` (root barrel) pulled into context-sdk's graph via context-sdk.ts:22 -> broke validateContext via tsx -e. Fixed by vendoring (TASK).
prior documentation of the same tsx-eval + SDK-root-barrel hazard class

impact: Silent degradation of the orchestrator's tsx -e verification harness (used constantly for validateContext/contextState/state checks); the gate (npm test) does not catch it because tsx --test resolves the barrel. Each new SDK util needed by a tsx-eval-reachable module risks re-breaking it.
proposed_resolution: Rule: tsx-eval-reachable modules (anything imported by context-sdk/block-api/context) must NOT statically import the SDK root barrel for VALUES. Vendor the needed util locally (as done for truncateHead, TASK), or import from a resolving SDK subpath if the SDK adds one. Type-only imports are fine (erased). Optional hardening: a check/test that greps tsx-eval-reachable modules for root-barrel value-imports.
